stance: Benchmark-aware large-cap core with a measured tilt to multi-signal value+momentum+rising-revision names (energy/materials/power-capex), deliberately taming the quant baseline's extended-momentum and capacity-thin chases.
experience_note: I am watching whether the rising-revision + cheap energy/materials cluster (ONGC, NMDC, NATIONALUM, COALINDIA) actually delivers over the next 1-2 quarters versus the extended-momentum names I deliberately passed on (MTARTECH, HFCL). If the most extended single-signal momentum names keep compounding and my multi-force value-momentum tilt lags by a wide margin over a full quarter, that argues my caution on capacity/beta was wrong and I should give cross-sectional momentum more independent weight. I would also revisit if the banking/financials core (where revisions are only neutral) drags persistently — that would say I am over-anchored to the benchmark. What would NOT change my mind: a single month of noise, or one extended name spiking further; I pre-register that I expect multi-signal confirmation to beat single-signal momentum on a risk-adjusted (drawdown-aware) basis, not necessarily on raw return.
guardrail_notes: dropped 16 non-priceable/unknown ticker(s): CIPLA, DRREDDY, ZYDUSLIFE, LUPIN, DMART, SBILIFE, ICICIGI, ICICIPRULI
